_comment: Every model resolved by core/src/model/Registry.cpp is classified into one of two tiers:
- fast: WASM-safe, interactive. Native<->WASM parity is GUARANTEED (|delta|=0 within the
parity-check tolerance) by the determinism guard, which runs every fast-tier config
- hifi: high-fidelity native path, GOLDEN-checked. Selected by config where a model has a
fast counterpart (e.g. 6dof vs 6dof_hifi, ekf vs imm, radar vs radar_pheno, round
The determinism guard (scripts/determinism-guard.mjs) and the unit test
postproc/tests/test_tiers.py read THIS file. It is authoritative: adding a fast-tier model to
the registry without listing it here (and giving it a fast-tier config that exercises it) fails
the guard, so parity coverage always tracks the fast-tier model set.
models: per registry family, model-key -> tier.
fast_tier_configs: configs the guard runs through parity (each must be |delta|=0).
hifi_configs: configs covered by the golden harness (postproc/gncpost/golden.py) instead.
config_models: maps each fast-tier config to the fast-tier model keys it exercises, so the
guard can prove every fast-tier model is reachable from at least one parity-checked config.
